summary:
The described bias occurs when patient populations are selectively included based on availability in medical records, leading to an overrepresentation of exposures—a hallmark of selection bias. This type of bias distorts estimates by excluding individuals who may have different exposure patterns outside healthcare settings.  

Final Answer: D

Only summarize Agent2’s actual reasoning without adding external information.

The described scenario involves selecting patients solely from hospital records, which skews exposure probabilities and results in non-representative samples—an accurate description of selection bias. Selection bias emerges because specific groups exposed to risk factors are disproportionately represented due to record-keeping practices within hospitals. Final answer remains D.
